Narrative_Meaning: Queens signify an older female, matriarchy, scandals, social events, curiosity, love, wisdom, sinister manipulations and betrayal.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Forge is the card of the workshop, or factory. It is a magical place, a womb of creation, where earth, air, water and fire are combined to create new objects like swords. The Forge is a place of sweat and hard work, but offers great rewards for this work.

Significator.Plot: The Forge Plot is a construction or creation plot. It is normal for such plots to be solely about the creation of a new technology or prototype, although there are always politics, market forces and other factors that revolves around a creation plot like this.
Significator.Side: The Forge is often useful as a Side Significator indicating that the side marked is trying to create something (or has created something).
Significator.Character:
The Forge Significator indicates a Character who is most likely a Smith or Artist. The card makes them even more so...

They Gain Yin by gathering resources, ores, fuel, and materials.

They Gain Yang by planning, drawing, and designing, writing notes on papers, preparing resources, etc.

They Gain Chi by creating Descendants (helping to pay off some of the costs of the Creation).

They also gain a +1 Success Level when they are creating.
Significator.Scene: The Forge Scene is often used for Descendant Creation (or shopping). A Yarn-Teller can play the Forge to allow themselves an Item Creation or Shopping Ordeal/Test or a Descendant Gain, they can also be used for personal Revelations by artist and craftsman Characters, as they work in their workshop.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Geometry specifically how they generate Yang and creates a situation that allows them to generate Yang this way.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook for the Character is a loss of something that can be replaced. Perhaps they have broken their sword, or their base is burnt down. The Conflict may drive them to replace this loss, or it may be part of a Villain's plan.
Yarn.Gain: Temporal, Material and Economic Power (Power Descendant)
Yarn.Fray_Description: The Ordeal is about the Character acquiring a Descendant. Maybe they go shopping and hunt around for a deal, but usually they make one themselves (or summon one magically, perhaps). The point is that they have to beat a Summoning / Creation difficulty somehow.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Either during the Fray or after it, the PCs will discover that they require something that they do not have. It might be an ingredient, or component, a specific person or piece of information. The point is, they cannot succeed without this thing.

NPC.Description: The Alchemist, also known as the Mad Scientist or Inventor likes to Craft new things, but they are not always as useful as the Alchemist thinks they will be.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PC improves or replaces a Descendant with an improved version, additional Nimbeds or Umbrals may be added to make the Descendant more powerful.
Yarn.History_Description: The Forge indicates that the current situation has its roots in a past of creation. Often some object or treasure was created in the past, a sword of kings forged and placed within a stone, or a ring of power, for example, but sometimes it is about the creation of a location or pact.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Forge indicates that currently the character is working steadily towards their goals. The work is usually hard, complex, and slow-going but they still feel productive.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Forge indicates that the solution to the current situation is some innovation, perhaps a new strategy, tactic, contraption, device, weapon, spell, technology, gambit, or gimmick that can be used to save the day. Often this is a new application of an old idea, such as using trebuchets which were designed to lob stones and break walls down, to launch dead animals into a city over the walls, or barrels of flaming pitch instead.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about a piece of equipment, treasure or device. A physical (Prop) Descendant that may be required or just desired by the Character. This Descendant is also sometimes known as a Plot Device, Plot Coupon, fetch-quest object, the Dooberry, Big Dumb Object, and the Weenie.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information that answers a Why question, like why are they doing it, why is it that way, or why won't the enemy leave their fortress?
Revelations.Alternate_Description: How something was built, or is being built can be very important, just think about giant space stations and the construction plans for them...
Revelations.Detail_Description: Practical details are details you can act on, who cares that the Arch-Mage's Tower is constructed in the Lorican Style, instead the practical details are that it is in a swamp, and can only be approached by boat.

Obstacle.Description: The way is ahead is blocked by heat, there is probably smoke or flame too, or it may be scorching desert asphalt.

Tarot.Normal: +2 Success Levels to all actions to do with technology, creativity, craft, creation and fixing things while atop pile.
Tarot.Crossed: +2 Failure Levels to all actions to do with technology, creativity, craft, creation and fixing things while atop pile.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 2 Obstacles in every Stage and 3 in all Court Stages
Ordeal.Stage_Description: A game of chicken, remember the facts, listen, pray, or race / sprint / speed
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Get through the Stages as fast as possible, if the Character has natural flight this is just a flight, otherwise they may swing from a rope / spider-web / lianna, or have to balance, climb and jump from roof-top to rooftop.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces rush the Ebbing enemy, closing to melee distance as quickly as possible.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A Femme (or Homme) Fatale is a seductive enemy, that will manipulate and single out weaker members, to eliminate them. Usually equivalent of PC in power, although often a Monster of some type.
  Facet    Monster  
Awe ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Terror — The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psych attacks and Negative Psych effects to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically draw and play Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) cards as a Psych Attack on any Character that sees them make any attack. They must take Awe Draw cards from their Monster Pool to defend, Soak or Heal any Attack made upon them. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.
Craft ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Maker — The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle / NPC Yarn card). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Scores and card Pips equal to the Creator’s Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced).
Orthodox ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Tyrant — The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of “the system” (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster add Orthodox Draw cards to their Monster Pool every time they are cruel, ruthless, authoritarian or demanding. Tyrants can add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels on another Character’s actions if they order them to do something. Tyrants can also add Monster Pool Size Score Orthodox Failure Levels to an enemy action for a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. The Tyrant must discard half of their their Monster Pool if they are ignored, forgotten or scorned.
Quiet ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Hush — The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 Score / Pip for each card in their Monster Pool when they try to act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl, including a Psych attack), it must play at least half of the Monster Pool on the action. Hush Monsters may add cards to their Monster Pool based on how long it has been since the last time they made a noise as a Yarn Extend Duration (e.g. After being silent for a few seconds add a card, after a scene of silence add two. Be silent for a week and add 6 cards to the Pool). Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream to create a Duck attack.
Virtue ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Friendly — The Monster is a “Friendly” monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some people. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or whenever they are attacked. A friendly Monster protects their friends from harm, and must play cards from their Monster Pool to defend, protect, or heal them. Friendly monsters may add a number of Failure Levels equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced to any enemy that tries to target them or their friends. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score (rolled Die or otherwise) to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.
Zeal ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Celestial — The Monster is a Celestial, a creature of divine or supernatural nature, often associated with a particular divinity, occasionally a political association, they often act as heralds of greater powers. Celestial beings include Anthropomorphic Personifications of social constructs like, Death, War, and Wyrd herself. When acting according to their nature, following their belief system, performing their duties, or following orders, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool. They automatically add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action to do with their nature, duty, travel, or communication. They also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to actions specific to their nature and duty. Celestial Monsters can only manifest a physical incarnation by playing cards directly from their Monster Pool on all physical actions. The monster could be an angel, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even one of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or Dragon configurations. Queens may also be used to create Chimeras.

Stress.Description: Stress can make monsters of us all, but worse still Stress can make Monsters, not just mountains, out of molehills.
Stress.Rules: The Character must pay Chi equal to the Character’s current Stress as Yarn. This Chi is used to purchase a new Character that can be a Force-of-Nature Extra with Monster Facets ((Awe, Craft, Orthodox, Quiet, Virtue, Zeal). These "Monsters" may actually be physical issues such as natural disasters, or they may be aspects of the Character's own Psyche, given hallucinatory or supernatural form. Then again they can be the Character themselves, unaware in their Stress-induced black-outs what they are doing. Other times the Character will project their problems on an actual Monster, tying their Arcs together. The only catharsis available to a Character here is to defeat, or at least survive the Monster, which will relieve Stress equal to the Monster Facet Boon.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can lead to new necessities in a Character’s life, these become the mothers of invention, with the Character creating some new device or technique to deal with the necessity. Sometimes the trauma itself is the necessity, with the mind coming up with some invention as the trauma is occuring, other times the invention may be some safety system to prevent this kind of trauma from occuring in the future.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma converts all Stress the Character currently has into Yarn-equivalent Chi that can only be used to create Prototype Descendants.

Age.Description: An Age of Industry is occurring. During this age a society is working, often maufacturing some item, or providing some service, that is well desired far beyond the society, producing an enormous amount of trade and wealth. Things are obviously positive economically, with close to full employment within the society and a sense that there is more work to do than time or available people-power. Societies and the individuals they are made from get rich during this age, even the poorest members of the society will be well off compared to another society.
Age.Rules: The entire society has an economic and industrial boom, often driven by a single product or service that the society may have a monopoly on. Such booms often have a limited duration, after which the society may move to another industry or service, or enter a new age.
